question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription where the historical retriever never completes (e.g., due to a DB timeout being very large), causing the concatWith() to block the live flux indefinitely, and since the safetyCheck fires after 1s on a separate thread, both threads remain permanently occupied, exhausting the bounded elastic pool and freezing fund processing?
